title: [FIRRTL] Printed Type of Subindex is Inconsistent
body:
In FIRRTL Dialect, the type of a subfield is `(original type) -> new type`. However, the type of a subindex is `(original type)`. This makes the pretty-printed format kind of weird to look at. Change the type of subindex to match the function-like syntax of subfield.

Consider:

```
circuit MuxChain :
  module MuxChain :
    input a: {a: UInt<1>}
    input b: UInt<1>[2]

    node a_a = a.a
    node b_0 = b[0]
```

This is printed as:

```mlir
module {
  firrtl.circuit "MuxChain"  {
    firrtl.module @MuxChain(in %a: !firrtl.bundle<a: uint<1>>, in %b: !firrtl.vector<uint<1>, 2>) {
      %0 = firrtl.subindex %b[0] : !firrtl.vector<uint<1>, 2>
      %1 = firrtl.subfield %a(0) : (!firrtl.bundle<a: uint<1>>) -> !firrtl.uint<1>
      %a_a = firrtl.node interesting_name %1  : !firrtl.uint<1>
      %b_0 = firrtl.node interesting_name %0  : !firrtl.uint<1>
    }
  }
}
```

It would be more readable as:

```
%0 = firrtl.subindex %b[0] : (!firrtl.vector<uint<1>, 2>) -> !firrtl.uint<1>
```

h/t @rwy7 for the suggestion.
